title: 1: Code Maintainability

annotations.description: N/A: No Description Required.
annotations.evidence: N/A: No Evidence Required.
annotations.justification: N/A: No Justification Required.

title: 1.1: Removes the --useoldmanifestkinds flag from the Makefile generate command

annotations.description: The generate target in apps/investigations/Makefile must not pass --useoldmanifestkinds to grafana-app-sdk generate. This deprecated flag is incompatible with the current App SDK manifest format.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch Makefile diff: line 11 removes '--useoldmanifestkinds' from the generate command.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states the Makefile 'needs to stop using the deprecated --useoldmanifestkinds flag.' [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] apps/investigations/Makefile currently passes --useoldmanifestkinds to the SDK generate command, which forces the old manifest format. [5: Conclusion] Any correct solution must remove --useoldmanifestkinds from the Makefile generate command.

title: 1.2: Adds --genoperatorstate=false to the Makefile generate command

annotations.description: The generate target in apps/investigations/Makefile must pass --genoperatorstate=false to grafana-app-sdk generate. This flag suppresses automatic generation of operator state status types for kinds that do not use them.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch Makefile diff: line 16 adds '--genoperatorstate=false' to the generate command.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'operator state evaluation is not needed for these resources' and the Makefile should 'use the current App SDK generation approach.' [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] In the Grafana App SDK, --genoperatorstate=false is the mechanism to prevent re-generation of operator status types during code generation. Without this flag, running make generate would recreate the removed status files. [3: Logical Necessity] Removing status types from generated code requires also preventing their re-generation on the next code generation run. [5: Conclusion] Any correct solution must add --genoperatorstate=false to the Makefile generate command.

title: 1.3: Restructures manifest.cue to organize kinds under a versions block

annotations.description: The apps/investigations/kinds/manifest.cue file must define a versions block (e.g., versions: { "v0alpha1": { kinds: [...] } }) instead of the current flat kinds array. Codegen settings should be specified within the version block.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch manifest.cue diff: lines 133-149 replace the flat 'kinds: [...]' array with a 'versions: { "v0alpha1": { codegen: {...}, kinds: [...] } }' structure.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'the manifest takes ownership of versioning, codegen settings, and organizing the kinds under their respective versions.' [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] The current manifest.cue uses a flat kinds array with no version structure, which is the deprecated old manifest format. Other apps in the codebase (e.g., playlist, alerting/notifications) already use the versions block pattern. [5: Conclusion] Any correct solution must restructure manifest.cue to organize kinds under a versions block.

title: 1.4: Removes the nested versions block from the Investigation kind in investigation.cue

annotations.description: The Investigation kind definition in apps/investigations/kinds/investigation.cue must not contain a nested versions block with codegen options. The schema should be defined directly under the kind, not nested inside versions/v0alpha1/schema. Fields like group, apiResource, current, and codegen that belong to the manifest should also be removed.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch investigation.cue diff: lines 27-60 remove the nested 'versions: { "v0alpha1": { codegen: {...}, schema: {...} } }' structure, and lines 61-71 show the spec schema directly under the kind definition.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'each kind file only declares what is unique to it, its kind name, plural name, and schema.' [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] investigation.cue currently nests the schema inside versions/v0alpha1/schema and includes group, apiResource, current, and codegen settings that are not unique to the kind. [5: Conclusion] Any correct solution must remove the nested versions block so the kind file only contains kind-specific declarations.

title: 1.5: Removes the nested versions block from the InvestigationIndex kind in investigationindex.cue

annotations.description: The InvestigationIndex kind definition in apps/investigations/kinds/investigationindex.cue must not contain a nested versions block with codegen options. The schema should be defined directly under the kind, not nested inside versions/v0alpha1/schema. Fields like group, apiResource, current, and codegen that belong to the manifest should also be removed.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch investigationindex.cue diff: lines 81-101 remove the nested 'versions: { "v0alpha1": { codegen: {...}, schema: {...} } }' structure, and lines 106-121 show the spec schema directly under the kind definition.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'each kind file only declares what is unique to it, its kind name, plural name, and schema.' [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] investigationindex.cue currently nests the schema inside versions/v0alpha1/schema and includes group, apiResource, current, and codegen settings that are not unique to the kind. [5: Conclusion] Any correct solution must remove the nested versions block so the kind file only contains kind-specific declarations.

title: 1.6: Renames the Investigation kind identifier to be version-aware in investigation.cue

annotations.description: The CUE identifier for the Investigation kind in apps/investigations/kinds/investigation.cue must include a version suffix (e.g., investigationV0alpha1 or investigationv0alpha1) so the manifest can reference it unambiguously under a specific version.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch investigation.cue diff: line 28 renames the identifier from 'investigation' to 'investigationV0alpha1'.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'the kind identifiers should be version aware so the manifest can reference them unambiguously.' [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] Other apps in the codebase (e.g., alerting/notifications) use version-aware identifiers like receiverv0alpha1 in their kind CUE files. [5: Conclusion] Any correct solution must rename the Investigation kind identifier to include version information.

title: 1.7: Renames the InvestigationIndex kind identifier to be version-aware in investigationindex.cue

annotations.description: The CUE identifier for the InvestigationIndex kind in apps/investigations/kinds/investigationindex.cue must include a version suffix (e.g., investigationIndexV0alpha1 or investigationIndexv0alpha1) so the manifest can reference it unambiguously under a specific version.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch investigationindex.cue diff: line 82 renames the identifier from 'investigationIndex' to 'investigationIndexV0alpha1'.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'the kind identifiers should be version aware so the manifest can reference them unambiguously.' [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] Other apps in the codebase (e.g., alerting/notifications) use version-aware identifiers like receiverv0alpha1 in their kind CUE files. [5: Conclusion] Any correct solution must rename the InvestigationIndex kind identifier to include version information.

title: 1.8: Removes the Status field from the Investigation struct in investigation_object_gen.go

annotations.description: The Investigation Go struct in apps/investigations/pkg/apis/investigations/v0alpha1/investigation_object_gen.go must not contain a Status field (e.g., 'Status InvestigationStatus').
annotations.evidence: Gold patch investigation_object_gen.go diff: lines 197-198 remove 'Status InvestigationStatus `json:"status" yaml:"status"`' from the Investigation struct.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'all status related machinery should be cleaned up across the generated Go code.' [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] The Investigation struct currently has a Status InvestigationStatus field that is unused — no operator evaluates status for this resource. [5: Conclusion] Any correct solution must remove the Status field from the Investigation struct.

title: 1.9: Removes the Status field from the InvestigationIndex struct in investigationindex_object_gen.go

annotations.description: The InvestigationIndex Go struct in apps/investigations/pkg/apis/investigations/v0alpha1/investigationindex_object_gen.go must not contain a Status field (e.g., 'Status InvestigationIndexStatus').
annotations.evidence: Gold patch investigationindex_object_gen.go diff: lines 364-365 remove 'Status InvestigationIndexStatus `json:"status" yaml:"status"`' from the InvestigationIndex struct.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'all status related machinery should be cleaned up across the generated Go code.' [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] The InvestigationIndex struct currently has a Status InvestigationIndexStatus field that is unused — no operator evaluates status for this resource. [5: Conclusion] Any correct solution must remove the Status field from the InvestigationIndex struct.

title: 1.10: Removes the UpdateStatus method from investigation_client_gen.go

annotations.description: The InvestigationClient in apps/investigations/pkg/apis/investigations/v0alpha1/investigation_client_gen.go must not have an UpdateStatus method.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch investigation_client_gen.go diff: lines 168-184 remove the entire UpdateStatus method from InvestigationClient.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'all status related machinery should be cleaned up across the generated Go code.' [3: Logical Necessity] The UpdateStatus method depends on the InvestigationStatus type and the Status field on the struct. Removing the Status field requires removing UpdateStatus to avoid compilation errors. [5: Conclusion] Any correct solution must remove the UpdateStatus method from InvestigationClient.

title: 1.11: Removes the UpdateStatus method from investigationindex_client_gen.go

annotations.description: The InvestigationIndexClient in apps/investigations/pkg/apis/investigations/v0alpha1/investigationindex_client_gen.go must not have an UpdateStatus method.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch investigationindex_client_gen.go diff: lines 335-351 remove the entire UpdateStatus method from InvestigationIndexClient.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'all status related machinery should be cleaned up across the generated Go code.' [3: Logical Necessity] The UpdateStatus method depends on the InvestigationIndexStatus type and the Status field on the struct. Removing the Status field requires removing UpdateStatus to avoid compilation errors. [5: Conclusion] Any correct solution must remove the UpdateStatus method from InvestigationIndexClient.

title: 3: Artifact Cleanup

annotations.description: N/A: No Description Required.
annotations.evidence: N/A: No Evidence Required.
annotations.justification: N/A: No Justification Required.

title: 3.1: Deletes the investigation_status_gen.go file

annotations.description: The file apps/investigations/pkg/apis/investigations/v0alpha1/investigation_status_gen.go should be deleted. It defines InvestigationStatus, InvestigationstatusOperatorState, and related types that are no longer referenced after status removal.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch investigation_status_gen.go diff: shows 'deleted file mode 100644', removing all 44 lines.
annotations.justification: [3: Logical Necessity] After removing the Status field from the Investigation struct and the UpdateStatus method from InvestigationClient, the types defined in investigation_status_gen.go (InvestigationStatus, InvestigationstatusOperatorState, etc.) become dead code. [4: Qualitative Requirement] Leaving orphaned type definitions increases maintenance burden and could cause confusion. [5: Conclusion] Any correct solution should delete investigation_status_gen.go.

title: 3.2: Removes status subresource handling from GetSubresources in investigation_object_gen.go

annotations.description: The GetSubresources method on Investigation in apps/investigations/pkg/apis/investigations/v0alpha1/investigation_object_gen.go should return an empty map instead of including a 'status' entry.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch investigation_object_gen.go diff: lines 206-209 update GetSubresources from returning a map with 'status': o.Status to returning an empty map[string]any{}.
annotations.justification: [3: Logical Necessity] After removing the Status field from the Investigation struct, the GetSubresources method cannot reference o.Status without a compilation error. [4: Qualitative Requirement] Subresource accessors should accurately reflect the resource's actual subresources. [5: Conclusion] Any correct solution should update GetSubresources to return an empty map.

title: 3.3: Removes status subresource handling from GetSubresources in investigationindex_object_gen.go

annotations.description: The GetSubresources method on InvestigationIndex in apps/investigations/pkg/apis/investigations/v0alpha1/investigationindex_object_gen.go should return an empty map instead of including a 'status' entry.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch investigationindex_object_gen.go diff: lines 372-376 update GetSubresources from returning a map with 'status': o.Status to returning an empty map[string]any{}.
annotations.justification: [3: Logical Necessity] After removing the Status field from the InvestigationIndex struct, the GetSubresources method cannot reference o.Status without a compilation error. [4: Qualitative Requirement] Subresource accessors should accurately reflect the resource's actual subresources. [5: Conclusion] Any correct solution should update GetSubresources to return an empty map.

title: 4: Negative Rubrics

annotations.description: N/A: No Description Required.
annotations.evidence: N/A: No Evidence Required.
annotations.justification: N/A: No Justification Required.

title: 4.1: Removes spec schema content from investigation.cue

annotations.description: Checks that the solution does NOT delete the spec schema fields (such as title, createdByProfile, hasCustomName, collectables, viewMode) from the Investigation kind definition in apps/investigations/kinds/investigation.cue. A 'Yes' rating means the agent incorrectly removed the data model.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch investigation.cue diff: lines 61-71 preserve all spec fields (title, createdByProfile, hasCustomName, isFavorite, overviewNote, overviewNoteUpdatedAt, collectables, viewMode) after the restructuring.
annotations.justification: [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] The spec schema in investigation.cue defines the Investigation API data model used by both the frontend plugin and backend services. These fields represent the API contract for Investigation resources. [4: Qualitative Requirement] The refactor is structural (moving versioning to the manifest) — it must not alter the data model itself. [5: Conclusion] Any correct solution must NOT remove spec schema fields from investigation.cue.

title: 4.2: Removes spec schema content from investigationindex.cue

annotations.description: Checks that the solution does NOT delete the spec schema fields (such as title, owner, investigationSummaries) from the InvestigationIndex kind definition in apps/investigations/kinds/investigationindex.cue. A 'Yes' rating means the agent incorrectly removed the data model.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch investigationindex.cue diff: lines 106-121 preserve all spec fields (title, owner, investigationSummaries) after the restructuring.
annotations.justification: [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] The spec schema in investigationindex.cue defines the InvestigationIndex API data model used by both the frontend plugin and backend services. These fields represent the API contract for InvestigationIndex resources. [4: Qualitative Requirement] The refactor is structural (moving versioning to the manifest) — it must not alter the data model itself. [5: Conclusion] Any correct solution must NOT remove spec schema fields from investigationindex.cue.
